title: Port Helix Navigators
content:
Hugo Gallant (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as captain of the Port Helix Navigators when they won the winter cup finals in 1980.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the winter cup finals, Hugo Gallant remained with the Port Helix Navigators for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Legacy.

Historians of the Port Helix Navigators cite Hugo Gallant's 1980 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Juniper Reach Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Early life and college career.

Hugo Gallant played college basketball in Juniper Reach before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as captain.

Personal life.

Hugo Gallant retired to Juniper Reach and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1982.
